issue.title: 🚨 "Database Error - Cannot Load Data" (indexedDB) when session is closed and reopened with autostart enabled
issue.body:
**Version Used**
17.2.4 (Linux Mint Cinnamon, Flatpak)

**Expected Behavior**
When logging out of a Linux Mint session and logging back in, with Super Productivity set to autostart, the app should open normally and load data without errors.

**Current Behavior**
After logging out of the Linux Mint (Cinnamon) session and logging back in, the app (launched automatically at session start) displays the following error:

"Database Error - Cannot Load Data
Super Productivity cannot open its database. This may be caused by:
- Low disk space
- Temporary file lock (try closing other tabs)
- Storage corruption
Technical details: Internal error opening backing store for indexedDB.open."

The error occurs consistently on every logout/login cycle. Workaround: manually closing the app, waiting a few minutes, then relaunching it starts correctly.

**Steps to Reproduce**
1. Install SP via Flatpak on Linux Mint Cinnamon
2. Set Super Productivity to autostart at session login
3. Open a session with SP running
4. Log out of the session (Menu → Log out)
5. Log back in
6. SP launches automatically and immediately shows the indexedDB error

Tested multiple times — behavior is consistent.

Note: Similar errors have been reported in older issues (#375, #419, #955) but none identified this specific trigger (logout/login cycle with autostart). Closing SP manually before logging out prevents the error.

**Your Environment**
- Version: 17.2.4
- OS: Linux Mint Cinnamon
- Install method: Flatpak (com.super_productivity.SuperProductivity)
- Sync: Dropbox (3 devices)

### Can you reproduce this reliably?

Yes, I can reproduce it reliably.

### Console Output

```shell

```

### documentation gap

- [ ] The documentation/[wiki](https://github.com/super-productivity/super-productivity/wiki) is unclear or incorrect for this case.
- [ ] There should be a note added to the documentation/[wiki](https://github.com/super-productivity/super-productivity/wiki) for this case.
- [ ] I can help improve the documentation.

fidelityNote: Cold-start in this repo, same as #7219/#7188.
